title: Cross-Tenant Data Leakage via Browser Cache
story: As a Salesforce administrator, I want the application to always load data for my authenticated tenant, so that I never see another organization's data due to browser caching.
description:
A cross-tenant data leakage issue was observed in production where a user authenticated as one Salesforce org (FundCanna) but the app loaded data from a different org (PBO). The issue appeared to be related to browser caching passing through a different team context than the authenticated user.

### Behavior
- User logged in as FundCanna org
- DataTools Pro loaded PBO org data instead
- In a different browser, FundCanna loaded correctly
- Issue was intermittent and difficult to reproduce

### Architecture Context
The app uses a `usedByTeam` trait that automatically injects the current team ID into the WHERE clause of each query. The issue may have been caused by cached team context from a previous session.

### Resolution
Closed as unable to reproduce — the video evidence was lost (wrong recording was shared). The `usedByTeam` trait should prevent this issue. Further investigation needed if it recurs.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated user always sees data from their own tenant
Browser caching does not cause cross-tenant data leakage
usedByTeam trait correctly injects current team ID on all queries
middleware_changes: Investigated usedByTeam trait for potential caching issues with team ID injection in query WHERE clauses.
implementation_notes: Uses usedByTeam trait to auto-inject team_id into queries. Issue could not be reproduced. Video evidence was lost (wrong recording). Closed pending recurrence.
comment_summary: Waqar investigated but couldn't reproduce the issue. The video link was inaccessible, and the replacement video was for a different task. Ryan acknowledged the recording was lost and agreed to close the ticket since the issue couldn't be reproduced.
